creator: bdrewery@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Mark Millard from comment #3)
> (In reply to w.schwarzenfeld from comment #2)
> 
> I'm confused. I wrote the Description and Comment #1.
> There is nothing else here for me to provide feedback
> relative to. Has something related changed somewhere
> that I should be looking at?
> 
> I'll note that I no longer have a zfs based environment
> so I do not have the context to test zfs specific settings
> any more.

They were asking for maintainer feedback but didn't say "maintainer".

Anyway I don't think this bug exists anymore.

The manpage documents this order for poudriere.conf file loading. You can
use 'unset' in the appropriate override file to undo any ZPOOL/BASEFS/NO_ZFS,
etc.

   Create optional poudriere.conf
     You can also specify an optional poudriere.conf that is pulled in depending on the build.  Any of the following are allowed and will all be used in the order shown:

           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<setname>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<tree>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<jailname>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<tree>-<setname>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<jailname>-<tree>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<jailname>-<setname>-poudriere.conf
           /usr/local/etc/poudriere.d/<jailname>-<tree>-<setname>-poudriere.conf
